name: Salmon Fillets with a Horseradish Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the sauce: Heat the cream in a pan over medium heat until it reaches a gentle simmer, about 2 minutes. Remove from heat and whisk together Dijon mustard, English mustard, horseradish, lemon juice, and cover; set aside.
2. Prepare the salmon: Using tweezers, remove any remaining bones. Cut into 4 equal portions, season each piece, and dredge with flour.
3. Prepare the crust: In a bowl, mix creamed horseradish, egg yolk, breadcrumbs, and parsley. Dip the top of each salmon piece in the mixture, ensuring it is well coated. Then dip the coated side into the breadcrumb mixture; shape by gently pressing with hands.
4. Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F/Gas 4. In a heavy-based frying pan, heat oil until very hot. Add salmon, crust-side down, and butter. Cook for about 3 minutes or until breadcrumbs are crisp. Turn over salmon, placing crust-side up in an ovenproof dish; bake for 5 minutes.
5. Stir chives into the sauce just before serving. Reheat briefly over low heat without boiling.
6. Arrange buttered spinach on warmed plates and top with salmon. Drizzle sauce over the fish and serve immediately.
